$comment: knext adapter deploy-test manifest (#89, ADR-0007 A3-2/A3-3, #147).
Consumed by vercel/next.js's run-tests.js via NEXT_EXTERNAL_TESTS_FILTERS.
Manifest layering is VERSION-DEPENDENT: at v16.0.3 test/get-test-filter.js
loaded EXACTLY ONE manifest path (no comma-split); v16.2.0+ DOES support
comma-separated manifest merging (mergeManifests: suites/include/exclude
union). We deliberately KEEP the single-file mirror for now — one file is the
complete, reviewable selection and the summary's excluded-count stays trivial —
so THIS file mirrors Next's own test/deploy-tests-manifest.json base set
(include/exclude AND suites) and ADDS knext's architectural exclusions on top.
Switching to comma-layering upstream's file is a recorded ADR-0007 follow-up.
version MUST be 2: get-test-filter.js only understands the legacy (no-version)
format and version===2; any other numeric version throws 'Unknown manifest
version'. The prior version:1 + object-shaped exclude list THREW at module load,
which is why every shard ran 0 tests (run 28314927507: passed:0 failed:0).
v2 semantics: a test file is RUN iff it matches an `include` glob and matches no
`exclude` glob (or is listed verbatim in `suites`). `suites` maps a file to its
per-case {failed,flakey} exclusions — populated below with (a) upstream's own
mirrored skips and (b) knext-OBSERVED flaky quarantines, added only from
OBSERVED failures with evidence, never pre-emptively. Every knext-observed
entry MUST have a matching `$knextQuarantines` ledger record (mechanism,
cross-run evidence, upstream provenance) — guarded by
tests/deploy-manifest.test.ts — and should be re-tested for removal on every
NEXTJS_REF bump.
HONESTY: this is an HONEST LEDGER, NOT a fake green. knext does NOT yet pass the
official suite; the compat-matrix official-suite row stays ❌ until a real
green nightly (ADR-0007 A3-3). The `$knextExclusions` ledger below names every
knext-SPECIFIC architectural exclusion with a rationale; the architectural
globs in `rules.exclude` are derived from it. Next's own (upstream) excludes are
carried verbatim from its deploy-tests-manifest.json and are NOT knext debt.
The exclude list must GROW FROM OBSERVED FAILURES and SHRINK toward zero. Do NOT
pre-emptively exclude tests to manufacture a green run.
suites (#147 A3-3 triage): the `failed` entries are mirrored VERBATIM from
vercel/next.js@v16.2.0 test/deploy-tests-manifest.json — upstream-known-failing
at v16.2.0: next.js itself skips them against its OWN deploy target, so running
them against knext books upstream noise as knext failures. Mirroring upstream's
own skips is honest and is NOT knext debt. Re-mirror whenever NEXTJS_REF bumps.
The nine prefetch-family `flakey` entries (segment-cache: search-params,
prefetch-layout-sharing, refresh, stale-time, per-page-dynamic-stale-time,
vary-params; plus app-client-cache/client-cache.parallel-routes,
app-prefetch/prefetching and optimistic-routing) are knext-OBSERVED
quarantines — see
`$knextQuarantines` for the per-entry mechanism, cross-run evidence and
upstream provenance. Quarantine bar: at least one FINAL (post-retry) file
failure across the full-run record; single-attempt wobbles absorbed by
in-run retry stay un-quarantined. Settings audit (round 4): upstream's
test-deploy-adapter lane runs the IDENTICAL per-case 60s timeout (hardcoded
individualTestTimeout), -c 2, ubuntu-latest, 3 attempts — this class wobbles
at full env parity and upstream ledgers it the same way.
#214 / ADR-0007 graduation addendum §d — FAMILY-LEVEL QUARANTINE: the runtime-
prefetch/navigation-timing flake family (60s hardcoded-individualTestTimeout hangs
in createRouterAct flows under -c 2 CPU contention; root cause fixed upstream in
vercel/next.js#95301, post-v16.2.0) is quarantined at FILE level via verbatim
rules.exclude entries, each backed by a level:"file" $knextQuarantines record
(mechanism + multi-run evidence + upstream provenance). Bar for membership:
>=1 knext FINAL post-retry failure with the family signature AND upstream
provenance (#95301 and/or an upstream suite-skip PR). Re-test the whole family
on the first NEXTJS_REF containing #95301. Everything else stays per-case.

cases: segment cache (search params) stores prefetched data by its rewritten search params, not the original ones
$knextQuarantines.mechanism: 60s jest timeout awaiting the middleware-rewritten full-prefetch response via createRouterAct (act includes: 'Search param: rewrittenSearchParam'). Serving layer exonerated by local repro: the exact rewritten full-prefetch request (RSC:1, through the fixture's middleware rewrite) against the real e2e-deploy.sh deployment (published next@16.2.0 + the shipped @knext/core adapter) returns 200 in ~76ms WITH 'rewrittenSearchParam' in the body, stream complete, vary/cache headers intact. The hang is client/CDP runtime-prefetch scheduling under CI shard load, not knext serving.
$knextQuarantines.evidence: run 28593534713: hung 3/3 attempts (same case, 60001ms each); run 28590478386: failed 2 attempts then RECOVERED on in-run retry — cross-run wobble, not deterministic debt.
$knextQuarantines.provenance: Upstream quarantines the runtime-prefetch deploy family wholesale: vercel/next.js@v16.2.0 test/deploy-tests-manifest.json marks ~34 'runtime prefetching …' cases flakey in segment-cache/prefetch-runtime.test.ts; THIS file additionally carries upstream's own in-file deploy-mode skip ('FIXME: search params seem to be dropped from the resume render when deployed', if (!isNextDeploy)). FAMILY ROOT CAUSE (#214, ADR-0007 §d): vercel/next.js#95301 (merged 2026-07-02, post-v16.2.0) fixes the client segment-cache race behind this exact signature — a locked navigation reused an in-flight runtime-prefetch entry without tracking it, drained without awaiting it, and read the unresolved shell, so the awaited content never surfaced and the case hit the bare 60s timeout; upstream's own PR: 'The race only lost under CPU contention, which is why it reproduced in the prod flake-detection job on slow containers but almost never locally.' Promoted to a file-level quarantine after per-case extension stopped converging (runs 28700392845 + 28701712403: a DIFFERENT family member finaled each run). First removal candidate: the first NEXTJS_REF containing #95301.

cases: app dir - prefetching should not unintentionally modify the requested prefetch by escaping the uri encoded query params

$knextQuarantines.mechanism: MIXED, honestly split: the uri-encoded case is an ASSERTION diff — an un-retried, immediate hasElementByCssSelector('#accordion-to-dashboard') check right after .click() (prefetching.test.ts:371) observes false when the client navigation isn't instant under shard load; the SUBSTANTIVE assertions of that test (the %20-encoded prefetch is issued and reused — both wrapped in retry()) PASS, so the encoding behavior itself is verified correct on knext. The two loading-state cases are the family's 60s createRouterAct hangs. Pre-fix run 28578203671 also failed its prefetch-priority cases, which have passed in every post-fix run (excluded from this quarantine).
$knextQuarantines.evidence: run 28597872225: FINAL failure (uri-encoded case failed identically 3/3; att1 also hung the two loading-state cases); run 28593534713: 2-attempt wobble (uri-encoded + a loading-state case each attempt) then RECOVERED; run 28590478386: 1-attempt wobble (all three cases) then recovered; run 28596005486: file PASSED — cross-run wobble on identical code.

FAMILY ROOT CAUSE (#214, ADR-0007 §d): vercel/next.js#95301 (merged 2026-07-02, post-v16.2.0) fixes the client segment-cache race behind this exact signature — a locked navigation reused an in-flight runtime-prefetch entry without tracking it, drained without awaiting it, and read the unresolved shell, so the awaited content never surfaced and the case hit the bare 60s timeout; upstream's own PR: 'The race only lost under CPU contention, which is why it reproduced in the prod flake-detection job on slow containers but almost never locally.' Promoted to a file-level quarantine after per-case extension stopped converging (runs 28700392845 + 28701712403: a DIFFERENT family member finaled each run). First removal candidate: the first NEXTJS_REF containing #95301.

cases: prefetch={true} with instant route also disables full prefetch when instant is on a layout, not the page
prefetch={true} with instant route does not include dynamic content in the prefetch when the target route has instant
$knextQuarantines.mechanism: 60s jest timeouts in createRouterAct-driven instant-route prefetch flows, alternating per attempt (run 28612654960: 'also disables full prefetch…' hung atts 1+3 at 60000ms while 'does not include dynamic content…' hung att 2 and passed elsewhere) — the runtime-prefetch family's interleaving-race signature, zero assertion diffs. NOT a Bucket-1/3/4 serving failure: the same run's server-log tails for this fixture carry no exception.
$knextQuarantines.evidence: run 28612654960 (KNEXT_RUNTIME=bun, #188 round 2): FINAL failure, alternating cases across 3 attempts; run 28607626868 (first bun run): file failed 1 attempt then RECOVERED on in-run retry (2/2, shard 3) — cross-run wobble, bun-lane evidence for both.
$knextQuarantines.provenance: Upstream marks the runtime-prefetch deploy family flakey wholesale (vercel/next.js@v16.2.0 test/deploy-tests-manifest.json, segment-cache/prefetch-runtime.test.ts, ~34 'runtime prefetching …' cases) — instant-route prefetch exercises exactly that client scheduling mechanism. FAMILY ROOT CAUSE (#214, ADR-0007 §d): vercel/next.js#95301 (merged 2026-07-02, post-v16.2.0) fixes the client segment-cache race behind this exact signature — a locked navigation reused an in-flight runtime-prefetch entry without tracking it, drained without awaiting it, and read the unresolved shell, so the awaited content never surfaced and the case hit the bare 60s timeout; upstream's own PR: 'The race only lost under CPU contention, which is why it reproduced in the prod flake-detection job on slow containers but almost never locally.' Promoted to a file-level quarantine after per-case extension stopped converging (runs 28700392845 + 28701712403: a DIFFERENT family member finaled each run). First removal candidate: the first NEXTJS_REF containing #95301.

cases: app-dir - server-actions-redirect-middleware-rewrite.test should redirect correctly in edge runtime with middleware rewrite
$knextQuarantines.mechanism: 60s jest timeout awaiting an EDGE-runtime server-action redirect through a middleware rewrite; zero assertion diffs and ZERO server-side exceptions in the surfaced server-log tails (run 28616072395). Cross-run wobble on the bun lane (fails/passes whole runs), so ledgered as flakey — but honestly noted: the hang shape overlaps the documented Bun ≤1.3.14 edge-sandbox outbound-fetch gap (next/dist/compiled/edge-runtime bundled-undici fetch never resolving; the middleware-rewrite proxy path exercises the same sandbox), which likely decides which runs lose the race. Re-examine when Bun ships the 1.4 fixes.
$knextQuarantines.evidence: run 28616072395 (KNEXT_RUNTIME=bun, #188 round 3): FINAL failure, same case 3/3 attempts at 60000ms; run 28607626868 (first bun run): FINAL failure, same case (Bucket-2 investigate list); run 28612654960 (round 2): file PASSED — cross-run wobble, bun lane only (Node lane green).
$knextQuarantines.provenance: Not in upstream's flakey ledger; bun-lane-only observation. Mechanism family: the Bun edge-sandbox outbound-fetch gap documented in PR #189 (middleware-fetches-with-any-http-method, app-static POST-fetch case) — the deterministic members of that family stay RED and unledgered; this case alone wobbles run-to-run.

cases: cached navigations caches runtime-prefetchable content from a navigation for instant second visit
cached navigations defers fallback params to the runtime stage
cached navigations includes static params in the cached static stage
cached navigations serves cached static segments instantly on the second navigation
cached navigations caches static segments when navigating to a known route without a prefetch
$knextQuarantines.mechanism: 60s jest timeouts in createRouterAct-driven cached-navigation/runtime-prefetch flows, alternating per attempt (run 28618585946: att1 'defers fallback params…' + 'caches runtime-prefetchable content…' hung; att2 only 'caches runtime-prefetchable content…'; att3 'serves cached static segments…' + 'includes static params…' — cases that hung one attempt passed the next) — the runtime-prefetch family's interleaving-race signature, zero assertion diffs, ZERO server-side exceptions in the surfaced server-log tails.
$knextQuarantines.evidence: run 28618585946 (KNEXT_RUNTIME=bun, #188 round 3 verify): FIRST FINAL failure, alternating cases across 3 attempts; run 28612654960 (round 2): 1-attempt wobble ('caches static segments when navigating to a known route without a prefetch') then RECOVERED on in-run retry; earlier bun runs: file PASSED — cross-run wobble, quarantined only after the final-post-retry bar was met.
$knextQuarantines.provenance: Upstream marks the runtime-prefetch deploy family flakey wholesale (vercel/next.js@v16.2.0 test/deploy-tests-manifest.json, segment-cache/prefetch-runtime.test.ts, ~34 'runtime prefetching …' cases) — cached navigations exercise exactly that segment-cache runtime-prefetch scheduling mechanism. FAMILY ROOT CAUSE (#214, ADR-0007 §d): vercel/next.js#95301 (merged 2026-07-02, post-v16.2.0) fixes the client segment-cache race behind this exact signature — a locked navigation reused an in-flight runtime-prefetch entry without tracking it, drained without awaiting it, and read the unresolved shell, so the awaited content never surfaced and the case hit the bare 60s timeout; upstream's own PR: 'The race only lost under CPU contention, which is why it reproduced in the prod flake-detection job on slow containers but almost never locally.' Promoted to a file-level quarantine after per-case extension stopped converging (runs 28700392845 + 28701712403: a DIFFERENT family member finaled each run). First removal candidate: the first NEXTJS_REF containing #95301. Upstream skip history, cited honestly: vercel/next.js#92199 (2026-04-15) it.skip'd ALL its cases as too flaky, then vercel/next.js#93798 (2026-05-13) REVERTED those skips — the file is fully live at canary. The revert PREDATES the #95301 root-cause fix (2026-07-02) and our pinned v16.2.0 predates both, so the race is un-fixed in our lane; this entry's membership therefore rests on the #95301 mechanism plus knext's OWN final-post-retry evidence (runs 28618585946, 28612654960), not on a live upstream skip.

cases: segment cache (basic tests) skips static layouts during partially static navigation
segment cache (basic tests) refreshes page segments when navigating to the exact same URL as the current location
segment cache (basic tests) navigate with prefetched data
$knextQuarantines.mechanism: 60s jest timeouts (the HARDCODED per-case individualTestTimeout in test/lib/e2e-utils — NEXT_E2E_TEST_TIMEOUT raises only the SETUP timeout) thrown from createRouterAct navigation/prefetch waits. Run 28701712403 shard 3/16: a DIFFERENT case hung on each of the 3 attempts ('skips static layouts…' at basic.test.ts:232, then 'refreshes page segments…' at :280, then 'navigate with prefetched data' at :57), 9/11 cases passing per attempt, zero assertion diffs, while the sibling -c 2 slot deployed+tested ~6 other fixtures during the first attempt (09:26:00–09:29:37) — the family's CPU-contention interleaving signature, not a knext serving failure (serving exonerated by the family's local repro, see the search-params entry).
$knextQuarantines.evidence: run 28701712403 (node lane, the #210 closure run, shard 3/16): FINAL failure past retries — 789 passed / 1 failed across all 16 shards, the lone red being this file; the file PASSED in every prior cited full run (28578203671, 28590478386, 28593534713, 28596005486, 28597872225, 28601386408) — cross-run wobble, newest family member.
$knextQuarantines.provenance: Upstream marks the runtime-prefetch deploy family flakey wholesale at v16.2.0 (test/deploy-tests-manifest.json, segment-cache/prefetch-runtime.test.ts, 33 cases) and this file already carries upstream's own per-case deploy skip (#90601). Promoted to a file-level quarantine after per-case extension stopped converging (runs 28700392845 + 28701712403: a DIFFERENT family member finaled each run). First removal candidate: the first NEXTJS_REF containing #95301.

$knextExclusions.rationale: knext targets the Node/Bun standalone server (output:'standalone'); it has no edge (V8-isolate) runtime. The Edge Runtime is architecturally out of scope — knext matches Vercel's compute layer, not its global edge (CLAUDE.md §8 bucket 1). compat-matrix row: Edge Runtime ❌.

Edge Middleware runs in the Edge Runtime, which knext does not provide. knext supports the (deprecated) Node middleware/proxy convention only; edge-middleware deploy tests assume the isolate runtime. Upstream-gated + architectural (CLAUDE.md §8). compat-matrix row: Edge Middleware ❌.
Partial Prerendering (PPR) is not adapter-standardizable yet (CLAUDE.md §6 Tier C, upstream-gated); knext does not implement the PPR resume/stream contract. compat-matrix row: PPR ❌.

Cache Components ('use cache' / cacheLife) are an evolving upstream API not yet stabilized for adapters (CLAUDE.md §6 Tier C). knext's Redis cache-handler does not implement the Cache Components protocol. compat-matrix row: Cache Components ❌.
